title: daucus latifolius
intro: Daucus latifolius, also known as wild fennel or daucus, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant's leaves, seeds, and roots possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, which aid in the relief of respiratory issues and digestive problems. The health benefits of Daucus latifolius include its ability to ease colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of menopause. Herbal preparations of Daucus latifolius may include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from its leaves, seeds, or roots, which can be consumed for medicinal purposes.
